commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Draft/protected maintainer stack review and missing direct snapshot-reuse proof require human handling rather than an automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or secret-handling defect was found; hosted feed trust remains a maintainer merge-risk decision. (sha=be8678a8055646657042662897dd2b5bef2b5950)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds feed-shaped official external plugin catalog loading with guarded hosted fetch, in-memory last-known-good snapshot fallback, ClawHub feed normalization, and focused catalog tests.

PR surface: Source +522, Tests +431, Other +5. Total +958 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a bug report. Source and focused tests cover the path, while the remaining gap is real behavior proof for hosted-snapshot reuse.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Draft/protected maintainer stack review and missing direct snapshot-reuse proof require human handling rather than an automated repair.

target.title: Add hosted catalog snapshot fallback
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## Journey ContextThis is step 3 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. The stack is [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) -> [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) -> [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877). #95846 made the bundled catalog feed-shaped. #95868 added guarded hosted fetch. This PR adds the last-known-good snapshot contract that makes the hosted path safe when the register endpoint is unchanged or temporarily unhealthy.## What This PR Does- Adds a narrow `HostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogSnapshotStore` interface.- Adds an in-memory snapshot store for tests and future runtime binding.- Writes the exact hosted response body plus hosted metadata and `savedAt` after a successful hosted load.- Uses a cached snapshot on 304, non-OK hosted responses, invalid hosted JSON/schema, or fetch failures.- Validates cached snapshot body checksum before use.- Validates cached snapshot schema before use.- Preserves `expectedSha256` pinning for snapshots, so a stale cached body cannot satisfy a caller-pinned checksum it does not match.- Falls back to the bundled catalog when no valid snapshot exists.## What This PR Intentionally Does Not DoThis PR does not choose a production persistence location, wire SQLite/runtime storage, enable the hosted loader by default, add source profiles, add auth, add signed envelopes, add regional feeds, or change search/install behavior. It defines the loader-level snapshot semantics that a later runtime-owned persistent store can bind to.## How To ReviewReview this as fallback semantics, not as the final storage implementation. The key behavior is: hosted feed first, validated last-known-good snapshot second, bundled catalog last. A snapshot write failure is intentionally non-fatal because a good hosted response should still be usable even if cache persistence fails.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (27 tests)- `timeout 420s pnpm tsgo:core:test`- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --uncommitted` initially found a real snapshot checksum-pinning issue; this PR fixes it. A later top-stack `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base refs/remotes/origin/main` found the hosted-fetch lazy import issue in #95868; that is also fixed and covered. Final top-stack review reported no discrete actionable correctness issues.  ## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The hosted loader reuses a last-known-good hosted snapshot when a later hosted fetch cannot provide an acceptable fresh payload.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr3` rebased through pushed PR3 head `be8678a805`, source test harness with in-memory snapshot store and mocked hosted responses.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts -t "uses the last known good snapshot"`
2. Final rebased-stack validation: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/marketplace.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 26 skipped (27)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

Observed result after fix:
The focused regression passed: after seeding a valid hosted snapshot, the loader returned the snapshot path instead of falling back to the bundled catalog when the fresh hosted response failed.

What was not tested:
This proof uses a mocked fetch path for deterministic snapshot failure behavior. Persistent SQLite snapshot storage is covered by the next PR.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

Final top-stack review reported no discrete actionable correctness issues.  ## Real behavior proof
